heading: Budget-related legislation must be within appropriate levels
label: 642 Budget-related legislation must be within appropriate levels
notes: References in TextThe Internal Revenue Code of 1986, referred to in subsec. (b)(2), is classified generally to Title 26, Internal Revenue Code.

CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to the general revision and enactment of Title 31, Money and Finance, by , , .section 1332 of Title 31Pub. L. 97–258, § 196 Stat. 877Sept. 13, 1982

Prior to amendment, section provided that new budget authority, new spending authority, and revenue legislation had to be within appropriate levels.Pub. L. 105–331990—Subsec. children.label: A
children.text: the enactment of that bill or resolution as reported;

children.label: B
children.text: the adoption and enactment of that amendment; or

children.label: C
children.text: the enactment of that bill or resolution in the form recommended in that conference report;

children.label: A
children.text: would cause the level of total new budget authority or total outlays set forth for the first fiscal year in the applicable resolution to be exceeded; or

children.label: B
children.text: would cause revenues to be less than the level of total revenues set forth for that first fiscal year or for the total of that first fiscal year and the ensuing fiscal years in the applicable resolution for which allocations are provided under .section 633(a) of this title

children.heading: Enforcement of social security levels in Senate
children.label: 3 Enforcement of social security levels in Senate
children.text: After a concurrent resolution on the budget is agreed to, it shall not be in order in the Senate to consider any bill, joint resolution, amendment, motion, or conference report that would cause a decrease in social security surpluses or an increase in social security deficits relative to the levels set forth in the applicable resolution for the first fiscal year or for the total of that fiscal year and the ensuing fiscal years for which allocations are provided under .section 633(a) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of subsection (a)(3), social security surpluses equal the excess of social security revenues over social security outlays in a fiscal year or years with such an excess and social security deficits equal the excess of social security outlays over social security revenues in a fiscal year or years with such an excess.

children.heading: Tax treatment
children.label: 2 Tax treatment
children.text: For purposes of subsection (a)(3), no provision of any legislation involving a change in chapter 1 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [ et seq.] shall be treated as affecting the amount of social security revenues or outlays unless that provision changes the income tax treatment of social security benefits.26 U.S.C. 1

children.heading: Exception in House of Representatives
children.label: c Exception in House of Representatives

children.text: the enactment of that bill or resolution as reported;
the adoption and enactment of that amendment; or
the enactment of that bill or resolution in the form recommended in that conference report;
